data.title: How food shaped Unilever for nearly a century
data.description:
Unilever has agreed to separate its food unit, home to Knorr and Hellmann's, and merge it with spice maker McCormick in a cash-and-stock deal valuing the British firm's food business at about $44.8 billion, the companies said on Tuesday.

The transaction marks the end of an era for Unilever, whose origins in the food business date back to a butter business from 1860.

Unilever will retain a 9% stake in the combined business though the company plans to sell it down one year after the deal closes.

The following timeline traces Unilever's evolution from a soap-and-margarine company into the world's second-largest packaged food company after Switzerland's Nestle :

pre

span

The Jurgens family, originally carpenters, begin selling butter received as payment for their work. As the trade proves highly profitable, they relocate to Oss in the Netherlands in 1860 to focus on building a butter business.

pre

pre

($1 = 0.8728 euros)

data.title: ZAWYA: M|A Group, Triumph Hotels partner to develop Triumph Pyramids Hotel in West Cairo
data.description:
Marwa Abo Almajd

Egypt’s M|A Group and Triumph Hotels Management have signed a strategic partnership to develop the Triumph Pyramids Hotel, a mixed-use hospitality project in West Cairo.

The project will be near the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM), positioning it within one of Egypt’s key tourism and investment zones, the companies said in a joint press statement.

The development will comprise hospitality, residential and commercial components, with Triumph Hotels responsible for management and operations, while M|A Group will act as the master developer and landowner.

Financial and project details weren't disclosed but the statement said delivery is expected within three years.

M|A Group’s Chairman Mohamed Ayoub said the project is intended to support Egypt’s tourism sector and contribute to the government’s target of attracting 30 million visitors annually by 2030.

Triumph Hotels Chairman Ehab Shouman added that the partnership aims to deliver a hospitality offering combining luxury accommodation with integrated services.

The project is expected to benefit from ongoing government initiatives to support and encourage hotel and tourism-related developments around the Grand Egyptian Museum and the Giza plateau, according to the statement.

(Writing by Marwa Abo Almajd; Editing by Anoop Menon) 

(anoop.menon@lseg.com)   

Subscribe to our Projects' PULSE newsletter that brings you trustworthy news, updates and insights on project activities, developments, and partnerships across sectors in the Middle East and Africa.

data.title: FutureFuel Cuts Dividend, Plans to Invest in Chemicals Business
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

FutureFuel cut its quarterly dividend to 1 cent a share effective for the second quarter and said it was redirecting capital to fund growth opportunities and repurchase shares.

The company's dividend for the first quarter was 6 cents a share.

FutureFuel said it will use the capital previously allocated to dividends to expand capacity, commercialize new products and "take advantage of the reshoring of specialty chemicals and advanced materials manufacturing to the United States."

The company said those investments would improve margins and accelerate growth in its chemicals segment.

The company also said it believes its stock is undervalued and reaffirmed its $25 million share-buyback authorization.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: TON Strategy reports $12.8M revenue, $356.8M digital assets; $148.4M net loss for 2025
data.description:
TON Strategy reported full-year 2025 results showing $12.8 million in revenue and a net loss of $148.4 million, reflecting a large fair value adjustment on its Toncoin holdings. As of December 31, 2025 the company held approximately 219.7 million units of Toncoin with a reported fair value of $356.8 million and cash and restricted cash of $39.7 million. Results follow implementation of the TON treasury strategy beginning in August 2025 and include continued operation of legacy businesses.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $12.8 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 (compared to $0.9 million in 2024).
  • Gross profit: $7.6 million for 2025 (compared to $0.7 million in 2024).
  • Total costs and expenses: $49.2 million for 2025 (compared to $12.5 million in 2024).
  • Loss from operations: $(36.4) million for 2025 (compared to $(11.6) million in 2024).
  • Net loss: $(148.4) million for 2025, which included a $(114.2) million net loss on crypto assets; net loss before income taxes $(148.6) million.

Business Highlights

  • Treasury strategy: Implemented TON treasury strategy beginning August 2025 and launched staking operations that ran through the fourth quarter as the first full quarter of staking.
  • Toncoin holdings and staking: Held approximately 219.7 million units of Toncoin at December 31, 2025, with approximately the same amount staked and a fair value of ~$356.8 million; earned ~2,185,286 units of Toncoin during 2025.
  • Custody and infrastructure: Maintained institutional custody and staking infrastructure using segregated validator structures and established custody, validator, reporting, and risk frameworks to support treasury operations.
  • Product and platform activity: Continued operation of legacy business units, including MARKET.live (multi-vendor livestream shopping) and LyveCom (AI-powered social commerce), contributing to revenue diversification.
  • Transparency and analytics: Launched an analytics dashboard to provide transparent access to key treasury and market metrics.

Original SEC Filing: TON Strategy Co [ TONX ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Meridian Reports Q4 Revenue $49.6M; FY2025 Revenue $182.9M, Net Loss Driven by Impairment
data.description:
Meridian reported fourth-quarter 2025 revenue of $49.6 million and record full-year 2025 revenue of $182.9 million, with gross profit of $28.5 million in Q4 and $103.5 million for the year. The company recorded a GAAP net loss of $88.4 million in Q4 and $92.0 million for FY2025, driven primarily by a $91.8 million non-cash goodwill and intangible asset impairment. Adjusted EBITDA was $4.6 million for Q4 and $19.4 million for the full year; cash at year-end was $18.1 million with net debt leverage of 0.86x.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Q4 2025 revenue $49.6 million (up 8% year-over-year); FY2025 revenue $182.9 million (up 21% vs. 2024).
  • Gross profit: Q4 2025 gross profit $28.5 million; FY2025 gross profit $103.5 million.
  • Operating income (loss): Loss from operations for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025 was $(96.2) million; Q4 2025 loss from operations was $(93.4) million.
  • Net income (loss): Q4 2025 net loss $(88.4) million (basic and diluted loss per share $7.09); FY2025 net loss $(92.0) million (basic and diluted loss per share $7.76).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: Q4 2025 Adjusted EBITDA $4.6 million; FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA $19.4 million. Cash at December 31, 2025 was $18.1 million and reported net debt $16.61 million (net debt leverage ratio 0.86x).

Business Highlights

  • Rebrand: The company completed a strategic rebrand unifying its corporate identity under the Meridian name.
  • Meridianbet Group: Delivered FY2025 revenue of $124.6 million (≈68% of company revenue) with ~70% gross margin; new customer registrations rose 72% year-over-year to 1.2 million, active users up 35%, depositors up 40%.
  • Expanse Studios: Expanded operator network from 184 to 1,344 active sites in FY2025 (630% growth) and achieved Q4 revenue growth of 435% year-over-year; filed for system certification in Ontario, Canada (pending regulatory approval).
  • RKings & Classics for a Cause: Segment revenue $43.8 million for FY2025 (up 35% year-over-year); raffle ticket volumes increased 137% year-over-year to 25.2 million, with estimated customer lifetime value per new registration up 19%.
  • GMAG / MexPlay: GMAG segment reported FY2025 revenue $14.5 million; MexPlay registrations grew 256% year-over-year in Q4 2025 with total active users of 32,308 in the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: Meridian Holdings Inc./NV [ MRDN ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: BIO-key Reports 2025 Revenue $6.1M, Q1’26 Preliminary Revenue $2.2M and $2.7M Cash
data.description:
BIO-key reported full-year 2025 revenue of $6.10 million and a net loss of $4.59 million, with cash and cash equivalents of $2.69 million at year-end. Preliminary Q1 2026 revenue is approximately $2.2 million, up 37% year-over-year, and the company expects stronger top-line growth and improved profitability in 2026. Management emphasized cost reductions, a strategic EMEA product transition, and strengthening defense, government and financial sector traction.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue for 2025: $6,095,117.
  • Gross profit for 2025: $4,722,080 with gross margin of 77.5%.
  • Operating loss for 2025: $(4,260,031).
  • Net loss for 2025: $(4,590,370) (basic and diluted loss per share: $(0.69)).
  • Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2025: $2,694,663; book value increased to $7,668,613.

Business Highlights

  • Preliminary Q1 2026 revenue: approximately $2.2 million, a 37% increase versus Q1 2025 and 80% above Q4 2025.
  • Completed strategic transition in EMEA to sell only BIO-key branded solutions (exiting prior Swivel Secure distribution), aimed at improving gross margins and rebuilding partner pipeline.
  • Strengthening traction in foreign military/defense and financial customers: over $2.2 million in military/defense revenue over the last 12 months and a significant Middle East deployment in Q4 2025.
  • Secured a $1.04 million one-year license renewal with a foreign retail bank and entered new partnerships across India, Vietnam, the Middle East/Africa, and the Nordics to expand international reach.
  • Launched new products including the ECOID III fingerprint scanner (FBI FAB 20 certified) and passwordless innovations showcased at the 2025 Gartner IAM Summit; formed a U.S. public sector partnership with TD Synnex Public Sector (DLT).

Original SEC Filing: BIO KEY INTERNATIONAL INC [ BKYI ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Unilever to Combine Foods Business With McCormick — WSJ
data.description:
By Lauren Thomas and Ben Dummett

Unilever agreed to combine its food business with McCormick in a deal that creates a new sauces-to-spices behemoth worth more than $65 billion, including debt.

The two companies announced the cash-and-stock deal Tuesday. Under the terms of the deal, Unilever and its shareholders are expected to own about 65% of the new, combined foods business. Unilever will also receive a one-time $15.7 billion cash payment.

The combined foods business will have annual revenues of about $20 billion. It will house Unilever's brands, including Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr soup mixes, with McCormick's red-capped bottled spices and French's yellow mustard.

The deal marks a major strategy shift by Unilever, leaving the London-based company to focus on beauty, personal-care and home products. The transaction does, though, add to the trend of consumer conglomerates streamlining their businesses.

The Wall Street Journal on Monday reported that the two companies were in advanced talks about a deal.

Confirming the deal Tuesday, the companies said the combination is expected to be structured as a so-called reverse Morris trust, which offers tax benefits.

Unilever has a market value of more than $130 billion, while McCormick's market value was a little over $14 billion as of Monday.

The companies said the new combined foods business would benefit from expanded global reach, more heft with retailers and greater resources to invest in innovation.

Write to Lauren Thomas at lauren.thomas@wsj.com and Ben Dummett at ben.dummett@wsj.com

data.title: McCormick investors like the $44.8 billion Unilever Foods deal, even though there's a catch
data.description:
By Tomi Kilgore

A published report indicated a deal, including a $16 billion cash component, could be announced when McCormick reported its first-quarter earnings, which occurred Tuesday

McCormick's stock surges amid reported talks to combine with Unilever's food business, but there's no mention of the deal in the U.S. company's earnings report.

Shares of McCormick & Co. rallied in early Tuesday trading, after the spice maker reported first-quarter earnings that beat analyst expectations, but didn't comment on what investors were actually waiting to hear about.

The real drama around the company (MKC), which sells French's mustard, Old Bay seasoning and Frank's RedHot sauces along with its spice range, is about the company's talks with U.K.-based Unilever (UL) (UK:ULVR) on a deal to combine with its foods business. Unilever is the parent of the Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr soup brands.

Unilever responded to a Wall Street Journal report about the deal by confirming that it was in talks with McCormick about a deal involving elements of its foods business. Unilever said there was no certainty a deal will be agreed, while the report said it could be announced as early as Tuesday, when McCormick was set to report earnings.

But McCormick left investors hanging, with no mention of the deal in the earnings release. A postearnings call with analysts is scheduled for 8 a.m. Eastern.

McCormick's stock climbed 4% in premarket trading. Prior to that gain, the stock was having a pretty bad month. It had dropped 24.4% in March through Monday, which put it on track for its biggest monthly decline since the record 39.5% one-month selloff in September 1980. The stock closed at an eight-year low on March 26.

Unilever's U.S.-listed shares tacked on 0.6% ahead of Tuesday's open. They had dropped 18.7% in March through Monday, which would be the worst month since they tumbled 20.2% in November 1999.

What McCormick did report Tuesday was that net sales for the quarter to Feb. 28 grew 16.7% from a year ago to $1.87 billion, which was well above the average analyst estimate compiled by FactSet of $1.79 billion. The margin of the sales beat was the widest since the second quarter of 2021.

The results included 25% sales growth in the consumer business to $1.15 billion, and a 6% increase in flavor solutions sales to $729 million.

Adjusted earnings per share, which excludes nonrecurring items, rose to 66 cents from 60 cents, to beat the FactSet consensus of 59 cents.

Looking ahead, the company affirmed its previous guidance ranges for sales growth of 13% to 17% and for adjusted EPS of $3.05 to $3.13.

-Tomi Kilgore

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: The First Criminal Trial in Alleged Russian Scheme that Snared Global Banks — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Bill Alpert

A French court held the first international criminal trial for a 2007 alleged Russian corruption scheme that cost Denmark's big bank $2 billion in fines and catalyzed the world's financial sanctions laws.

Paris prosecutors alleged yesterday that 58-year-old Russian citizen Dmitry Klyuev masterminded a 2007 scheme that stole $230 million from Russia's treasury. Charging documents said that corrupt cops raided and hijacked the largest Western hedge fund in Moscow, Bill Browder's Hermitage Capital. Impersonators then sought refunds of $230 million in capital-gains taxes that Hermitage had paid to Russia's government, according to the charging documents. Corrupt tax officials quickly complied, and the money disappeared into a bank controlled by Klyuev, prosecutors allege.

When Browder's tax expert Sergei Magnitsky reported the scheme to Russian authorities, he was arrested by the involved cops and later died after a beating by eight prison guards, according to Russia's Presidential Human Rights Council.

Browder has spent two decades seeking justice for Magnitsky's death. He successfully lobbied for laws in the U.S., the U.K. and European Union that provide financial sanctions against crooked foreign officials and human rights violators. These laws have become known as Magnitsky Acts, and were the models for today's wide use of individual sanctions in foreign policy. Under Magnitsky laws, Klyuev was barred from visiting or holding assets in seven countries.

Browder also spent years tracing the proceeds of the scheme, with the help of investigative journalists at Barron's and other publications. Barron's found over $10 million in prime Manhattan real estate that U.S. prosecutors then seized as proceeds in a 2013 civil money laundering case. Barron's also helped track cash smuggled to the U.S. through subsidiaries of Danske Bank, which in 2022 pleaded guilty to U.S. bank fraud charges and paid a $2 billion fine. Its stock sank 49% on the news.

Russia, for its part, said it couldn't find and recover the hundreds of millions taken from its Treasury as tax refunds.

France began investigating missing proceeds from the scheme in 2014. It issued an arrest warrant for Klyuev last August. The charges in today's trial allege that he laundered over 2 million euros from the theft to buy French jewelry, art, and yacht charters. Prosecutors asked for a fine of 4.5 million euros and a three-year prison sentence.

Klyuev wasn't represented in the criminal proceedings, and was tried in absentia. Barron's reached out to his onetime attorney, but received no comment.

French criminal trials can be done in a day , and this one was. The judge will announce the verdict at a June 8 hearing.

Browder takes some satisfaction at the long-in-coming trial.

"The Magnitsky investigation, which has been at the heart of major journalistic exposés over the past decade, exposed the machinery of Russian money laundering," he said. "Now, with French authorities and the U.S. Department of Justice identifying Dmitry Klyuev as the mastermind, we finally see a trial against the key perpetrator of the $230 million fraud uncovered by Sergei Magnitsky."

Write to Bill Alpert at william.alpert@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Elektros (OTC:ELEK) Unveils Strategic Breakthrough as Ludlow Research Issues Comprehensive Market Report
data.description:
SUNNY ISLES BEACH, FL / ACCESS Newswire / March 31, 2026 / Elektros, Inc. (OTC PINK:ELEK), an emerging innovator in electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure and energy solutions, is pleased to announce that Ludlow Research, a New York-based equity research firm, has issued a comprehensive research report on the Company. The report highlights Elektros' strategic pivot toward vertical integration within the global energy transition sector.

Report Highlights

  • Surging global lithium demand strengthens the Company's position within a critical commodity market

  • Newly patented multi-port charging technology establishes a meaningful competitive advantage

  • Energy efficiency consulting services provide diversified and scalable revenue streams

  • Anticipated rise in summer energy prices driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions

The Ludlow Research report emphasizes Elektros' dual-focus strategy of developing lithium mining resources while commercializing its proprietary EV charging technology. It also addresses the broader macroeconomic environment, noting that geopolitical instability in the Strait of Hormuz has contributed to rising oil prices approaching $100 per barrel. Analysts forecast sustained elevated energy costs through the summer, which may accelerate global adoption of energy-efficient infrastructure solutions.

A full copy of the report is available at:

https://www.ludlowresearch.com/reports/

About Ludlow Research

Ludlow Research is a New York-based equity research and advisory firm providing institutional-grade valuation analysis and strategic market intelligence. With over 25 years of capital markets expertise, the firm delivers independent research designed to support capital formation and high-level strategic decision-making.

About Elektros, Inc.

Elektros, Inc. (OTC PINK:ELEK) is focused on the development of artisanal hard-rock lithium mining operations in Sierra Leone, West Africa. The Company's strategy centers on lithium exploration, development, and the export of mined materials to refining partners in the United States.

Investor Relations: www.elektros.energy/investors

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements involve risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Factors include, but are not limited to, regulatory developments, operational execution, market conditions, and technological changes.
